8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler

15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5

17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
201	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
202	            )
203	        return logits
204	
205	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
206	        self,
207	        logits: torch.Tensor,
208	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
209	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
210	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
211	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
212	                                float("-inf"))
213	        return logits
214	
215	    def apply_bad_words(
216	        self,
217	        logits: torch.Tensor,
218	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
219	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
220	        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
221	            apply_bad_words(
222	                logits,
223	                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
224	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
225	            )
226	        return logits

11	from vllm.lora.request import LoRARequest
12	from vllm.multimodal.inputs import MultiModalKwargs, PlaceholderRange

17	from vllm.v1.pool.metadata import PoolingMetadata
18	from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import (BatchUpdateBuilder,
19	                                             MoveDirectionality,
20	                                             init_builtin_logitsprocs)

27	@dataclass
28	class CachedRequestState:
29	
30	    req_id: str
31	    prompt_token_ids: list[int]
32	    mm_inputs: list[MultiModalKwargs]
33	    mm_positions: list[PlaceholderRange]
34	    sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]
35	    pooling_params: Optional[PoolingParams]
36	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
37	
38	    block_ids: tuple[list[int], ...]
39	    num_computed_tokens: int
40	    output_token_ids: list[int]
41	
42	    mrope_positions: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
43	    mrope_position_delta: Optional[int] = None
44	
45	    lora_request: Optional[LoRARequest] = None
46	
47	    def __post_init__(self):
48	        self.num_prompt_tokens = len(self.prompt_token_ids)
49	
50	    @property
51	    def num_tokens(self) -> int:
52	        return self.num_prompt_tokens + len(self.output_token_ids)
53	
54	    def get_token_id(self, idx: int) -> int:
55	        if idx < self.num_prompt_tokens:
56	            return self.prompt_token_ids[idx]
57	        else:
58	            return self.output_token_ids[idx - self.num_prompt_tokens]
59	
60	
61	class InputBatch:
62	
63	    def __init__(
64	        self,
65	        max_num_reqs: int,
66	        max_model_len: int,
67	        max_num_batched_tokens: int,
68	        device: torch.device,
69	        pin_memory: bool,
70	        vocab_size: int,
71	        block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
72	        is_spec_decode: bool = False,
73	        logits_processing_needs_token_ids: bool = False,
74	    ):
75	        self.is_spec_decode = is_spec_decode
76	        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs
77	        self.max_model_len = max_model_len
78	        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens
79	        self.device = device
80	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
81	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
82	        self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids = (
83	            logits_processing_needs_token_ids)
84	
85	        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
86	        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}
87	
88	        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
89	        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
90	        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
91	        # need to be pinned.
92	        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
93	            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),
94	            device="cpu",
95	            dtype=torch.int32,
96	            pin_memory=False,
97	        )
98	        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
99	        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
100	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
101	        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
102	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
103	            (max_num_reqs, ),
104	            device="cpu",
105	            dtype=torch.int32,
106	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
107	        )
108	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
109	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
110	
111	        # Block table.
112	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
113	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
114	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
115	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
116	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
117	            device=device,
118	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
119	        )
120	
121	        # Sampling-related.
122	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
123	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
124	                                       device=device)
125	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
126	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
127	                                                  device="cpu",
128	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
129	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
130	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
131	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
132	
133	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
134	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
135	                                 device=device)
136	        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
137	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
138	                                            device="cpu",
139	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
140	        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
141	        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
142	
143	        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
144	                                 dtype=torch.int32,
145	                                 device=device)
146	        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
147	                                            dtype=torch.int32,
148	                                            device="cpu",
149	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
150	        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
151	        self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()
152	
153	        # IDs of requests which do not support spec decoding
154	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs: set[str] = set()
155	
156	        # Frequency penalty related data structures
157	        self.frequency_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
158	                                               dtype=torch.float,
159	                                               device=device)
160	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
161	            (max_num_reqs, ),
162	            dtype=torch.float,
163	            device="cpu",
164	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
165	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu = \
166	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
167	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
168	
169	        # Presence penalty related data structures
170	        self.presence_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
171	                                              dtype=torch.float,
172	                                              device=device)
173	        self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
174	                                                         dtype=torch.float,
175	                                                         device="cpu",
176	                                                         pin_memory=pin_memory)
177	        self.presence_penalties_cpu = self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy(
178	        )
179	        self.presence_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
180	
181	        # Repetition penalty related data structures
182	        self.repetition_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
183	                                                dtype=torch.float,
184	                                                device=device)
185	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
186	            (max_num_reqs, ),
187	            dtype=torch.float,
188	            device="cpu",
189	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
190	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu = \
191	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
192	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
193	
194	        # lora related
195	        self.request_lora_mapping = np.zeros((self.max_num_reqs, ),
196	                                             dtype=np.int32)
197	        self.lora_id_to_request_ids: dict[int, set[str]] = {}
198	        self.lora_id_to_lora_request: dict[int, LoRARequest] = {}
199	
200	        # req_index -> generator
201	        # NOTE(woosuk): The indices of the requests that do not have their own
202	        # generator should not be included in the dictionary.
203	        self.generators: dict[int, torch.Generator] = {}
204	
205	        self.num_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
206	        # NOTE(rob): num_prompt_logprobs only includes reqs
207	        # that are currently in the prefill phase.
208	        self.num_prompt_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
209	
210	        # To accumulate prompt logprobs tensor chunks across prefill steps.
211	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu: dict[str, LogprobsTensors] = {}
212	
213	        # Internal representation of per-step batch state changes, used for
214	        # reordering persistent batch and generating logitsprocs batch state
215	        # updates. Should reset each step.
216	        self.batch_update_builder = BatchUpdateBuilder()
217	
218	        # Define logits processors.
219	        # TODO(andy): logits processor list should be extensible via engine
220	        # constructor argument; for now the list is fixed.
221	        self.logitsprocs = init_builtin_logitsprocs(
222	            pin_memory_available=pin_memory,
223	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1,
224	            device=device)
225	
226	        # TODO convert this to LogitsProcessor
227	        self.has_allowed_token_ids: set[str] = set()
228	        # NOTE(lufang): In the mask tensor, if the corresponding token allowed,
229	        # the value is False. Since we use masked_fill_ to set -inf.
230	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
231	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
232	
233	        # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
234	        self.bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]] = {}
235	
236	        self.req_output_token_ids: list[Optional[list[int]]] = []
237	
238	        # This is updated each time the batch constituents change.
239	        self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()
240	
241	        self.pooling_params: dict[str, PoolingParams] = {}
242	
243	    @property
244	    def req_ids(self) -> list[str]:
245	        # None elements should only be present transiently
246	        # while performing state updates to the batch.
247	        return cast(list[str], self._req_ids)
248	
249	    def _get_next_add_index(self) -> int:
250	        if (req_index := self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()) is not None:
251	            # Fill the empty index.
252	            return req_index
253	        # Append to end
254	        return self.num_reqs
255	
256	    def _register_add_request(self, request: "CachedRequestState") -> int:
257	        """Track add-request operations"""
258	        req_index = self._get_next_add_index()
259	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
260	        params = (request.sampling_params
261	                  if request.sampling_params else request.pooling_params)
262	        self.batch_update_builder.added.append(
263	            (req_index, params, request.output_token_ids))
264	        return req_index
265	
266	    def add_request(
267	        self,
268	        request: "CachedRequestState",
269	    ) -> int:
270	        req_index = self._register_add_request(request)
271	
272	        req_id = request.req_id
273	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
274	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
275	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
276	        else:
277	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
278	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
279	
280	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index
281	
282	        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
283	        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
284	        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
285	        self.token_ids_cpu[
286	            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
287	        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
288	        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
289	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
290	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
291	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
292	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
293	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
294	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
295	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
296	
297	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
298	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
299	
300	        if sampling_params := request.sampling_params:
301	            if (self.is_spec_decode
302	                    and is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params)):
303	                self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.add(req_id)
304	            if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
305	                # Avoid later division by zero.
306	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
307	                self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
308	            else:
309	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
310	                self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
311	
312	            self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
313	            if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
314	                self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
315	            top_k = sampling_params.top_k
316	            if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
317	                self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
318	            else:
319	                top_k = self.vocab_size
320	            self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
321	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
322	                req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
323	            if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
324	                self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
325	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
326	                req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
327	            if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
328	                self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
329	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
330	                req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
331	            if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
332	                self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
333	
334	            # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
335	            # do not have their own generator.
336	            if request.generator is not None:
337	                self.generators[req_index] = request.generator
338	
339	            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
340	                self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
341	            if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
342	                self.num_prompt_logprobs[
343	                    req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
344	
345	            if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
346	                self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
347	                if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
348	                    # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
349	                    # False means we don't fill with -inf.
350	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(
351	                        self.max_num_reqs,
352	                        self.vocab_size,
353	                        dtype=torch.bool,
354	                        device=self.device)
355	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
356	                        self.max_num_reqs,
357	                        self.vocab_size,
358	                        dtype=torch.bool,
359	                        device="cpu")
360	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
361	                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
362	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
363	                    sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False
364	
365	            if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
366	                self.bad_words_token_ids[
367	                    req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
368	        else:
369	            assert request.pooling_params is not None
370	            self.pooling_params[req_id] = request.pooling_params
371	
372	        # Add request lora ID
373	        if request.lora_request:
374	            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
375	            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:
376	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id] = set()
377	
378	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = lora_id
379	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].add(request.req_id)
380	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request[lora_id] = request.lora_request
381	        else:
382	            # No LoRA
383	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
384	
385	        return req_index
386	
387	    def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
388	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().
389	        
390	        Args:
391	          req_id: request to remove
392	
393	        Returns:
394	          Removed request index, or `None` if `req_id` not recognized
395	        """
396	
397	        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
398	        if req_index is None:
399	            return None
400	        self.batch_update_builder.removed_append(req_index)
401	        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
402	        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None
403	
404	        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
405	        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
406	        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
407	        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
408	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.discard(req_id)
409	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
410	        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
411	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
412	        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
413	        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
414	        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
415	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)
416	
417	        # LoRA
418	        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
419	        if lora_id != 0:
420	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
421	            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
422	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
423	                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
424	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
425	
426	        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
427	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
428	            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
429	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
430	        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
431	        self.pooling_params.pop(req_id, None)
432	        return req_index
433	
434	    def swap_states(self, i1: int, i2: int) -> None:
435	        self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
436	            (i1, i2, MoveDirectionality.SWAP))
437	        old_id_i1 = self._req_ids[i1]
438	        old_id_i2 = self._req_ids[i2]
439	        self._req_ids[i1], self._req_ids[i2] =\
440	            self._req_ids[i2], self._req_ids[i1] # noqa
441	        self.req_output_token_ids[i1], self.req_output_token_ids[i2] =\
442	            self.req_output_token_ids[i2], self.req_output_token_ids[i1]
443	        assert old_id_i1 is not None and old_id_i2 is not None
444	        self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2] =\
445	            self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1]
446	        self.num_tokens[i1], self.num_tokens[i2] =\
447	            self.num_tokens[i2], self.num_tokens[i1]
448	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2] =\
449	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1]
450	        self.num_prompt_tokens[i1], self.num_prompt_tokens[i2] =\
451	            self.num_prompt_tokens[i2], self.num_prompt_tokens[i1]
452	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2] =\
453	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1]
454	        self.temperature_cpu[i1], self.temperature_cpu[i2] =\
455	            self.temperature_cpu[i2], self.temperature_cpu[i1]
456	        self.top_p_cpu[i1], self.top_p_cpu[i2] =\
457	            self.top_p_cpu[i2], self.top_p_cpu[i1]
458	        self.top_k_cpu[i1], self.top_k_cpu[i2] =\
459	            self.top_k_cpu[i2], self.top_k_cpu[i1]
460	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
461	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1]
462	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
463	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1]
464	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
465	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1]
466	
467	        # NOTE: the following is unsafe
468	        # self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], =\
469	        #     self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...]
470	        # instead, we need to temporiarily copy the data for one of the indices
471	        # TODO(lucas): optimize this by only copying valid indices
472	        tmp = self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...].copy()
473	        self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...] = self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...]
474	        self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...] = tmp
475	
476	        swap_dict_values(self.generators, i1, i2)
477	        swap_dict_values(self.bad_words_token_ids, i1, i2)
478	
479	        self.request_lora_mapping[i1], self.request_lora_mapping[i2] =\
480	            self.request_lora_mapping[i2], self.request_lora_mapping[i1]
481	
482	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
483	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1], \
484	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2] =\
485	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2], \
486	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]

489	    def condense(self) -> None:
490	        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
491	
492	        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
493	        filled.
494	
495	        Args:
496	          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.
497	
498	        Returns:
499	          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
500	          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
501	        """
502	        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
503	            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
504	            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
505	            return
506	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
507	        if num_reqs == 0:
508	            # The batched states are empty.
509	            self._req_ids.clear()
510	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
511	            return
512	
513	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
514	        # is sorted in descending order.
515	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
516	        while empty_req_indices:
517	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
518	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
519	                last_req_index -= 1
520	
521	            # Find the smallest empty index.
522	            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
523	            assert empty_index is not None
524	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
525	                break
526	
527	            # Move active request down into empty request
528	            # index.
529	            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()
530	            self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
531	                (last_req_index, empty_index,
532	                 MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL))
533	            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
534	            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
535	            assert req_id is not None
536	            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
537	            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
538	            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
539	            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
540	            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index
541	
542	            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
543	            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
544	                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
545	            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
546	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
547	                last_req_index]
548	            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
549	                last_req_index]
550	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
551	                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
552	            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
553	            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
554	                last_req_index]
555	            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
556	            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
557	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
558	                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
559	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
560	                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
561	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
562	                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
563	            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
564	            if generator is not None:
565	                self.generators[empty_index] = generator
566	
567	            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
568	                last_req_index]
569	
570	            # TODO convert these to LogitsProcessors
571	            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
572	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
573	                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
574	                        last_req_index]
575	
576	            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
577	                last_req_index, None)
578	            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
579	                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids
580	
581	            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
582	            last_req_index -= 1
583	
584	        # Trim lists to the batch size.
585	        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
586	        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]

588	    def refresh_metadata(self):
589	        """Apply batch updates, reset input batch at end of step
590	        
591	        * Apply batch add/remove/permute to logits procs' states
592	        * If batch state is modified, update sampling metadata
593	        """
594	        batch_update = self.batch_update_builder.get_and_reset(self.num_reqs)
595	        for logit_proc in self.logitsprocs.all:
596	            logit_proc.update_state(batch_update)
597	        if batch_update:
598	            self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
602	        if not self.all_greedy:
603	            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
605	        else:
606	            temperature = None
607	        if not self.no_top_p:
608	            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
609	        if not self.no_top_k:
610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,
649	            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
650	            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
651	            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
652	            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
653	            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
654	            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
655	            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
656	            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
657	            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
658	            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
659	        )

662	    def pooling_metadata(self) -> PoolingMetadata:
663	        if len(self.pooling_params) == 0:
664	            pooling_params = []
665	        else:
666	            # Note, for now this assumes that all request in the batch
667	            # are either sampling or pooling requests
668	            assert len(self.req_ids) == len(self.pooling_params)
669	            pooling_params = [
670	                self.pooling_params[req_id] for req_id in self.req_ids
671	            ]
672	
673	        return PoolingMetadata(
674	            prompt_lens=torch.from_numpy(

676	            prompt_token_ids=self.sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
677	            pooling_params=pooling_params,

680	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
682	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
683	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
684	            device="cpu",
685	            dtype=torch.int64,
686	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
687	        )
688	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
689	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
690	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
691	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
692	        # token_id of this value.
693	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
694	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
695	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
696	                                              non_blocking=True)

698	    def make_lora_inputs(
699	        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
700	    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
701	        """
702	        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
703	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
704	        Returns:
705	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
706	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
707	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
708	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
709	            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
710	        """
711	
712	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
713	        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
714	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
715	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
716	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
717	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
718	
719	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests

225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.

497	    @classmethod
498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,
500	        temperatures: list[float],
501	        top_ps: list[float],
502	        top_ks: list[int],
503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],
505	        frequency_penalties: list[float],
506	        repetition_penalties: list[float],
507	        prompt_tokens: list[array],
508	        output_tokens: list[array],
509	        vocab_size: int,
510	        device: torch.device,
511	        dtype: torch.dtype,
512	    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
513	        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
514	        # pinned memory.
515	        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
516	
517	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
518	
519	        if do_penalties:
520	            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
521	                prompt_tokens,
522	                vocab_size,
523	                device="cpu",
524	                dtype=torch.int64,
525	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
526	            )
527	            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
528	                output_tokens,
529	                vocab_size,
530	                device="cpu",
531	                dtype=torch.int64,
532	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
533	            )
534	        else:
535	            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
536	            prompt_t = empty_tensor
537	            output_t = empty_tensor
538	
539	        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
540	            temperatures,
541	            device="cpu",
542	            dtype=dtype,
543	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
544	        )
545	        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
546	            top_ps,
547	            device="cpu",
548	            dtype=dtype,
549	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
550	        )
551	        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
552	            min_ps,
553	            device="cpu",
554	            dtype=dtype,
555	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
556	        )
557	        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
558	            presence_penalties,
559	            device="cpu",
560	            dtype=dtype,
561	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
562	        )
563	        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
564	            frequency_penalties,
565	            device="cpu",
566	            dtype=dtype,
567	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
568	        )
569	        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
570	            repetition_penalties,
571	            device="cpu",
572	            dtype=dtype,
573	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
574	        )
575	        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
576	            top_ks,
577	            device="cpu",
578	            dtype=torch.int,
579	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
580	        )
581	        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
582	        # transfer to device.
583	
584	        return cls(
585	            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
586	            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
587	            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
588	            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
589	            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
590	                                                       non_blocking=True),
591	            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
592	                                                         non_blocking=True),
593	            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
594	                                                           non_blocking=True),
595	            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
596	            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
597	        )

199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

17	def model() -> LLM:

24	def test_n_gt_1(model):

36	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):

40	def test_penalties(model):
41	    """Check that we do not get errors if applied."""
42	
43	    params = SamplingParams(
44	        temperature=1.2,
45	        presence_penalty=1.2,
46	        frequency_penalty=1.2,
47	        repetition_penalty=1.2,
48	        min_p=0.5,
49	        top_p=0.5,
50	        top_k=3,

55	def test_stop(model):
56	    """Check that we respect the stop words."""
57	
58	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
59	    split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
60	
61	    STOP_IDX = 5
62	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop=split_text[STOP_IDX])
63	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
64	    new_split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
65	
66	    # Output should not contain the stop word.
67	    assert len(new_split_text) == STOP_IDX
68	
69	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0,
70	                            stop=split_text[STOP_IDX],
71	                            include_stop_str_in_output=True)
72	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
73	    new_split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
74	
75	    # Output should contain the stop word.
76	    assert len(new_split_text) == STOP_IDX + 1
77

79	def test_stop_token_ids(model):
80	    """Check that we respect the stop token ids."""
81	
82	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
83	
84	    stop_token_id_0 = output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[5]
85	    stop_token_id_1 = output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[6]
86	
87	    stop_token_ids = [stop_token_id_1, stop_token_id_0]
88	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids)
89	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
90	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == stop_token_id_0
91	
92	    stop_token_ids = [stop_token_id_0, stop_token_id_1]
93	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids)
94	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
95	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == stop_token_id_0

99	    """Check that detokenize=False option works."""
100	
101	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(detokenize=False))
102	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].token_ids) > 0
103	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].text) == 0
104	
105	    output = model.generate(

107	                               prompt_logprobs=3))
108	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].token_ids) > 0
109	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].text) == 0
110	
111	    prompt_logprobs = output[0].prompt_logprobs
112	    sampled_logprobs = output[0].outputs[0].logprobs
113	    assert len(prompt_logprobs) > 1
114	    assert len(sampled_logprobs) > 1
115	    for all_logprobs in (prompt_logprobs[1:], sampled_logprobs):
116	        for logprobs in all_logprobs:

121	def test_bad_words(model):
122	    """Check that we respect bad words."""
123	
124	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
125	    split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
126	
127	    bad_words_1 = " ".join(split_text[:2])
128	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, bad_words=[bad_words_1])
129	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
130	    new_text = output[0].outputs[0].text
131	    assert bad_words_1 not in new_text
132	
133	    bad_words_2 = new_text.split()[-1]
134	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0,
135	                            bad_words=[bad_words_1, bad_words_2])
136	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
137	    new_text = output[0].outputs[0].text
138	    assert bad_words_1 not in new_text
139	    assert bad_words_2 not in new_text

142	def test_logits_processor(model):
143	    """Check that we reject logits processor."""
144	
145	    # This sample logits processor gives infinite score to the i-th token,
146	    # where i is the length of the input sequence.
147	    # We therefore expect the output token sequence to be [0, 1, 2, ...]
148	    def pick_ith(token_ids, logits):
149	        logits[len(token_ids)] = float("inf")
150	        return logits
151	
152	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
153	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT,
154	                           SamplingParams(logits_processors=[pick_ith]))
155	
156	
157	def test_allowed_token_ids(model):
158	    """Check that we can use allowed_token_ids."""
159	
160	    TOKEN_ID = 10
161	    allowed_token_ids = [TOKEN_ID]
162	    output = model.generate(
163	        PROMPT, SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids))
164	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == TOKEN_ID
165	
166	    # Reject empty allowed_token_ids.
167	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
168	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=[]))
169	
170	    # Reject negative token id.
171	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
172	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=[-1]))
173	
174	    # Reject out of vocabulary.
175	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
176	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT,
177	                           SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=[10000000]))
178

180	def test_priority(model):
181	    """Check that we reject requests with priority."""
182	
183	    # Reject all allowed token ids
184	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
185	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, priority=[1])
186	
187	
188	def test_seed(model):
189	    """Check that seed impacts randomness."""
190	
191	    out_1 = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(seed=42))
192	    out_2 = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(seed=42))
193	    out_3 = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(seed=43))
194	
195	    assert out_1[0].outputs[0].text == out_2[0].outputs[0].text
196	    assert out_1[0].outputs[0].text != out_3[0].outputs[0].text

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

42	    prompt_token_ids = None
43	    frequency_penalties = None
44	    presence_penalties = None
45	    repetition_penalties = None
46	    # should use tensor
47	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())
48	
49	    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized
50	
51	    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
52	        default_factory=lambda: list())
53	
54	    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
55	    bad_words_token_ids = None
56	
57	    # Generator not supported by xla
58	    _generators: dict[int,
59	                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())
60	
61	    @property
62	    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
63	        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
64	        return self._generators
65	
66	    @classmethod
67	    def from_input_batch(
68	        cls,
69	        input_batch: InputBatch,
70	        padded_num_reqs: int,
71	        xla_device: torch.device,
72	        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
73	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
74	        """
75	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
76	
77	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
78	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
79	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
83	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
84	            xla_device: The XLA device.
85	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
86	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
87	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
88	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
89	        """
90	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
91	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
92	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
93	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
94	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
95	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
96	
97	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
98	
99	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
100	            # Pad value is the default one.
101	            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val
102	
103	        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
104	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
105	        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
106	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
107	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
108	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
109	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
110	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
111	
112	        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
113	        return cls(
114	            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
115	            to(xla_device),
116	            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
117	            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
118	            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
119	                xla_device),
120	            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
121	                xla_device),
122	            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
123	                xla_device),
124	            logprobs=needs_logprobs)
125

3	"""Sampler layer implementing TPU supported operations."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
10	from vllm.v1.sample.tpu.metadata import TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80	
81	    def gather_logprobs(
82	        self,
83	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
84	        num_logprobs: int,
85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
87	        """
88	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
89	
90	        Args:
91	          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
92	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
93	                        retain per token
94	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
95	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
97	                     with (num tokens) elements
98	
99	        Returns:
100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
101	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
102	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
103	        """
104	        # Find the topK values.
105	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
106	                                                 num_logprobs,
107	                                                 dim=-1)
108	
109	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
110	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
124	
125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits
146
